metadata.description: Android app UI automation flows via ADB with recording at three speed modes (slow, normal, fast)

challenges.name: Android: App Launch from Launcher
challenges.description: Launch the Yole Android app from the device launcher, verify the splash screen displays, the main activity starts, and the bottom navigation bar renders within acceptable time bounds at each speed mode.

steps.description: Install the debug APK on the connected device/emulator via ADB

steps.target: adb shell pm list packages | grep digital.vasic.yole
steps.description: Verify the package 'digital.vasic.yole' is installed on the device
Launch the Yole main activity via ADB activity manager

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys activity activities | grep mResumedActivity
steps.description: Wait for MainActivity to become the resumed (foreground) activity
Capture a screenshot of the app immediately after launch

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep bottom_navigation
steps.description: Verify the bottom navigation bar is present in the UI hierarchy
Verify the 'Files' tab label is displayed in the bottom navigation
Verify the 'Todo' tab label is displayed in the bottom navigation
Verify the 'QuickNote' tab label is displayed in the bottom navigation
Verify the 'More' tab label is displayed in the bottom navigation
Verify the app launched and became interactive within 5 seconds

assertions.message: APK must install on device
Main activity must start
Bottom navigation must render with all tabs
App must launch within 5 seconds

challenges.name: Android: Bottom Navigation (Files, Todo, QuickNote, More)
challenges.description: Tap each bottom navigation tab in sequence and verify each screen renders its expected content. Verify the selected tab indicator updates.

steps.description: Tap the 'Files' tab at the bottom-left of the navigation bar

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys activity fragments | grep FileBrowser
steps.description: Wait for the FileBrowser fragment to load into the foreground

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep file_browser_list
steps.description: Verify the file browser RecyclerView list is displayed

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep 'selected="true"' | grep 'Files'
steps.description: Verify the Files tab shows the selected indicator
Capture a screenshot of the Files tab view
Tap the 'Todo' tab in the bottom navigation bar

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys activity fragments | grep TodoList
steps.description: Wait for the TodoList fragment to load

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep todo_task_list
steps.description: Verify the Todo task list is displayed

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep 'selected="true"' | grep 'Todo'
steps.description: Verify the Todo tab shows the selected indicator
Tap the 'QuickNote' tab in the bottom navigation bar

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys activity fragments | grep QuickNote
steps.description: Wait for the QuickNote fragment to load

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep quicknote_editor
steps.description: Verify the QuickNote editor area is displayed
Tap the 'More' tab in the bottom navigation bar

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys activity fragments | grep MoreMenu
steps.description: Wait for the MoreMenu fragment to load

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep more_menu
steps.description: Verify the More menu options are displayed
Tap the 'Files' tab again to verify returning to the initial view

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep file_browser_list
steps.description: Verify the file browser list is displayed after returning to Files

assertions.message: Files tab must render file browser
Todo tab must render task list
QuickNote tab must render editor
More tab must render menu
Must return to Files tab cleanly

challenges.name: Android: FAB Actions (New File, Quick Note)
challenges.description: Tap the floating action button (FAB) to create a new file and a quick note. Verify the FAB menu expands, each action navigates to the correct editor, and the file is persisted.

steps.description: Tap the Files tab to ensure we start from the file browser screen

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep fab_button
steps.description: Wait for the FAB (floating action button) to appear on screen
Tap the floating action button to expand its menu options

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep fab_menu
steps.description: Wait for the FAB speed-dial menu to expand with action items

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep fab_new_file
steps.description: Verify the 'New File' FAB action item is visible in the expanded menu
Tap the 'New File' action from the FAB speed-dial menu

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep new_file_dialog
steps.description: Wait for the new file creation dialog to appear
Type the filename 'fab-test.md' into the file name field via ADB input
Tap the 'Create' button in the dialog to confirm file creation

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Wait for the editor to open with the new empty file

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Verify the editor area is displayed for the new file
Press the hardware Back button to return from editor to file browser

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep file_browser_list
steps.description: Wait for the file browser to re-appear after pressing Back
Tap the FAB again to expand the menu for the Quick Note action
Tap the 'Quick Note' action from the FAB speed-dial menu

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep quicknote_editor
steps.description: Verify the QuickNote editor is displayed after tapping Quick Note

assertions.message: FAB menu must expand on tap
New file action must create file and open editor
Quick note action must open QuickNote editor

challenges.name: Android: Document Creation with On-Screen Keyboard
challenges.description: Create a new Markdown document and type content using the on-screen keyboard. Verify keyboard appears, text is entered correctly, and the content is saved.

steps.description: Tap the Files tab to navigate to the file browser screen
Tap the floating action button to expand the creation menu
Tap the 'New File' action from the FAB menu
Type the filename 'mobile-doc.md' into the file name input field
Tap the 'Create' button to confirm the file creation

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Wait for the editor area to become active with the new empty document
Tap the center of the editor area to focus it and bring up the soft keyboard

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys input_method | grep mInputShown=true
steps.description: Wait for the on-screen soft keyboard to appear

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys input_method | grep mInputShown
steps.description: Verify the soft keyboard is visible on screen

steps.target: adb shell input text '# Mobile Document'
steps.description: Type the Markdown heading using ADB text input

steps.target: adb shell input keyevent KEYCODE_ENTER KEYCODE_ENTER
steps.description: Press Enter twice to create a blank line after the heading

steps.target: adb shell input text 'Typed on **Android** with the on-screen keyboard.'
steps.description: Type a paragraph with Markdown bold syntax

steps.target: adb shell input keyevent KEYCODE_ENTER KEYCODE_ENTER
steps.description: Press Enter twice for a new paragraph

steps.target: adb shell input text '- Buy groceries'
steps.description: Type the first Markdown list item
Press Enter to move to the next line

steps.target: adb shell input text '- Walk the dog'
steps.description: Type the second Markdown list item
Tap the Save button in the toolbar to persist the document

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep save_indicator
steps.description: Wait for the save indicator to confirm the file was written to storage

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep '# Mobile Document'
steps.description: Verify the editor displays the typed Markdown content
Press Back to dismiss the on-screen keyboard
Capture a screenshot of the completed document on Android

assertions.message: On-screen keyboard must appear when editor is tapped
Text must be entered correctly via keyboard
Content must be saved to storage

challenges.name: Android: Swipe Gestures and Scroll
challenges.description: Open a long document, verify smooth scrolling via swipe gestures. Test fling scrolling, precise scrolling, and scroll-to-top/bottom.

steps.description: Tap the Files tab to navigate to the file browser
Tap the long document in the file browser to open it in the editor

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Wait for the long document content to fully load in the editor
Capture a screenshot of the document at the top position
Perform a fast upward swipe from bottom to top to scroll the content down
Wait for the scroll animation to settle after the fast swipe

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area scrollY
steps.description: Verify the editor has scrolled down from the initial position
Perform a slow upward swipe for precise scrolling control
Verify the scroll position moved further down with the slow swipe
Perform a fast downward swipe to scroll the content back up
Verify the editor scrolled back up after the downward swipe
Perform a rapid fling gesture to scroll with momentum toward the bottom
Wait for the fling momentum animation to decelerate and stop
Capture a screenshot after the fling scroll to show the new position
Verify the fling scroll moved the document near the bottom

assertions.message: Swipe up must scroll content down
Swipe down must scroll content up
Fling gesture must scroll with momentum
Scrolling must be smooth without jank

challenges.name: Android: Settings Screen Navigation
challenges.description: Navigate to the Settings screen via the More tab, verify all setting categories render, toggle theme and editor settings, and verify changes take effect.

steps.description: Tap the 'More' tab in the bottom navigation to open the more menu

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep more_menu
steps.description: Wait for the More menu to render with menu items
Tap the 'Settings' item in the More menu to open settings

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys activity activities | grep SettingsActivity
steps.description: Wait for the Settings activity or fragment to become visible

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep settings_appearance
steps.description: Verify the 'Appearance' settings category is displayed

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep settings_editor
steps.description: Verify the 'Editor' settings category is displayed

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep settings_storage
steps.description: Verify the 'Storage' settings category is displayed
Capture a screenshot of the main settings screen
Tap the 'Appearance' category to open theme settings
Tap the 'Dark' theme option to switch to dark mode
Wait for the dark theme transition to complete
Capture a screenshot with dark theme applied
Tap the 'Light' theme option to switch back to light mode
Press Back to return from appearance settings to the main settings list
Tap the 'Editor' category to open editor-specific settings
Tap the line numbers toggle switch to change its state
Press Back to return from editor settings to the main settings
Press Back again to return from settings to the More menu

outputs.name: settings_rendered
outputs.description: Whether settings screen rendered all categories

assertions.message: Settings screen must render all categories
Theme changes must take immediate effect
Editor settings must toggle correctly

challenges.name: Android: Back Button Behavior
challenges.description: Test Android system back button behavior across navigation depth: from editor back to file list, from settings back to more menu, and from root activity to home.

steps.description: Tap the Files tab to ensure we start from the file browser
Tap the FAB to create a new file for the back navigation test
Tap the 'New File' action from the FAB menu
Type the filename for the back button test file
Tap Create to open the new file in the editor

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Wait for the editor to open with the new file

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Verify we are in the editor (deepest navigation level for this test)
Press the hardware Back button to go from editor back to file list

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep file_browser_list
steps.description: Wait for the file browser list to reappear after pressing Back

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep file_browser_list
steps.description: Verify the file browser list is displayed (back from editor worked)
Tap the More tab to navigate to the More menu
Tap Settings to navigate deeper into the settings screen

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep settings_screen
steps.description: Wait for the settings screen to render
Press Back to go from settings back to the More menu

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep more_menu
steps.description: Verify the More menu is displayed (back from settings worked)
Press Back from the root activity to go to the Android launcher

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys activity activities | grep -v digital.vasic.yole
steps.description: Verify the launcher is now the foreground activity (app exited)

outputs.name: back_from_editor
outputs.description: Whether back from editor returned to file list

outputs.name: back_from_settings
outputs.description: Whether back from settings returned to more menu

outputs.name: back_to_home
outputs.description: Whether back from root returned to launcher

assertions.message: Back from editor must return to file browser
Back from settings must return to more menu
Back from root must return to launcher

challenges.name: Android: Rotation Handling (Portrait/Landscape)
challenges.description: Open a document, rotate the device from portrait to landscape and back. Verify content is preserved, scroll position is maintained, and layout adapts correctly.

steps.target: adb shell settings put system accelerometer_rotation 0
steps.description: Disable auto-rotation so we can control orientation via ADB commands
Tap the test file in the file browser to open it in the editor

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Wait for the document content to fully load in the editor
Capture a screenshot of the document in portrait orientation before rotation

steps.target: adb shell settings put system user_rotation 1
steps.description: Rotate the device to landscape orientation (user_rotation=1)

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys display | grep mCurrentOrientation
steps.description: Wait for the display to confirm landscape orientation is active
Wait for the activity to recreate after the configuration change

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Verify the editor area is visible after rotation to landscape
Capture a screenshot of the document in landscape orientation
Verify the document content was preserved across the rotation to landscape

steps.target: adb shell settings put system user_rotation 0
steps.description: Rotate the device back to portrait orientation (user_rotation=0)
Wait for the activity to recreate after returning to portrait

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Verify the editor area is visible after returning to portrait
Capture a screenshot of the document back in portrait orientation
Verify the document content was preserved across the round-trip rotation

steps.target: adb shell settings put system accelerometer_rotation 1
steps.description: Re-enable auto-rotation to restore the device to its default state

outputs.name: content_preserved_landscape
outputs.description: Whether content survived rotation to landscape

outputs.name: content_preserved_portrait
outputs.description: Whether content survived rotation back to portrait

assertions.message: Layout must adapt to landscape orientation
Content must survive rotation to landscape
Content must survive rotation back to portrait

challenges.name: Android: Share Intent Handling
challenges.description: Send a share intent with text content to the Yole app via ADB. Verify the app opens, receives the shared text, and displays it in the editor.

steps.description: Force-stop the app to ensure the share intent launches it from a clean state

steps.target: adb shell am start -a android.intent.action.SEND -t text/plain --es android.intent.extra.TEXT '{{shared_text}}' digital.vasic.yole/.MainActivity
steps.description: Send a SEND intent with text/plain MIME type and the shared text extra to the Yole app

steps.target: adb shell dumpsys activity activities | grep mResumedActivity
steps.description: Wait for the Yole MainActivity to become the resumed foreground activity

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Wait for the editor area to appear with the shared text content

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Verify the editor area is displayed and contains content
Verify the shared text appears in the editor content area
Capture a screenshot showing the app received and displayed the shared text
Tap the Save button to persist the shared content to local storage

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep save_indicator
steps.description: Wait for the save indicator to confirm the shared text was saved

outputs.name: intent_received
outputs.description: Whether the share intent was received

outputs.name: text_displayed
outputs.description: Whether shared text appeared in editor

outputs.name: content_saveable
outputs.description: Whether shared content could be saved

assertions.message: Share intent must be received by the app
Shared text must appear in the editor
Shared content must be saveable

challenges.name: Android: File Browser Navigation
challenges.description: Navigate the file browser on Android: create a folder, enter it, navigate back using the toolbar up button, and verify the file listing updates correctly.

steps.description: Tap the Files tab to navigate to the file browser screen

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep file_browser_list
steps.description: Wait for the file browser list to render with the current directory contents
Long-press on an empty area of the file browser to trigger the context menu
Hold the long press for 1 second to trigger the context menu

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep context_menu
steps.description: Verify the context menu appeared with folder creation option
Tap 'New Folder' in the context menu

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep folder_name_field
steps.description: Wait for the folder name input dialog to appear
Type the folder name into the creation dialog input field
Tap the Create button to confirm the folder creation
Wait for the new folder to appear in the file browser listing
Verify the newly created folder is displayed in the listing
Tap the new folder to navigate into it

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep toolbar_title
steps.description: Verify the toolbar title shows the current folder name
Tap the toolbar Up/Back button to navigate to the parent directory
Verify the folder is still listed in the parent directory after navigating back
Capture a screenshot of the file browser showing the created folder

assertions.message: Folder must be created in file browser
Must navigate into folder
Must navigate back via toolbar up button

challenges.name: Android: Create Todo.txt Document
challenges.description: Create a todo.txt file on Android, enter tasks with priorities and projects using the on-screen keyboard. Verify task parsing and priority highlighting.

steps.description: Tap the Files tab to navigate to the file browser
Tap the floating action button to expand the creation menu
Tap the 'New File' action to create a new file

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep file_name_field
steps.description: Wait for the file name input dialog to appear
Type the filename 'android-tasks.todo' with the .todo extension
Tap Create to open the new file in the editor with todo.txt format

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Wait for the editor to become active with an empty todo.txt document
Tap the editor area to focus it and bring up the on-screen keyboard

steps.target: adb shell input text '(A) Fix Android layout bug +yole @mobile due:2026-03-22'
steps.description: Type a priority (A) task with project tag, context tag, and due date
Press Enter to move to the next line

steps.target: adb shell input text '(B) Add swipe gesture support +yole @ux'
steps.description: Type a priority (B) task with project and context tags
Press Enter to move to the next line

steps.target: adb shell input text 'x 2026-03-16 Implement bottom nav +yole @mobile'
steps.description: Type a completed task with the 'x' prefix and completion date
Tap the Save button in the toolbar to persist the todo.txt file
Wait for the save indicator to confirm the file was written to storage

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep priority_highlight_A
steps.description: Verify the priority (A) task is highlighted with the highest-priority color

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep project_tag
steps.description: Verify the +yole project tags are syntax-highlighted
Capture a screenshot of the todo.txt editor with parsed and highlighted tasks

outputs.name: file_created
outputs.description: Whether the todo.txt file was created

assertions.message: Todo.txt file must be created
Tasks must be parsed with priorities and tags
Priority levels must be highlighted

challenges.name: Android: Preview Mode Toggle
challenges.description: Open a Markdown document, switch to preview mode via the toolbar, verify HTML rendering, then switch back to editor mode and verify content integrity.

steps.description: Tap the Files tab to navigate to the file browser
Tap the test Markdown file to open it in the editor

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Wait for the Markdown content to fully load in the editor

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Verify the editor area is displaying the raw Markdown source
Capture a screenshot of the editor mode showing raw Markdown text
Tap the Preview toggle button in the toolbar to switch to preview mode

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep preview_webview
steps.description: Wait for the WebView-based preview to render the Markdown as HTML

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep preview_webview
steps.description: Verify the preview WebView is displayed with rendered HTML content
Capture a screenshot of the preview mode showing rendered HTML
Tap the Editor toggle button in the toolbar to switch back to editing mode

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Wait for the editor area to reappear with the raw Markdown source

steps.target: adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/tty | grep editor_area
steps.description: Verify the editor area is displayed and contains the original content
Verify the Markdown source is intact after the preview round-trip

outputs.name: preview_rendered
outputs.description: Whether preview mode showed rendered HTML

outputs.name: editor_restored
outputs.description: Whether editor content survived the round-trip

assertions.message: Preview must render formatted HTML in WebView
Editor content must be intact after preview round-trip
